name: Poza de Arco to Pozas de Loureza — circular by the Río Tamuxe
name_original: Poza de Arco a Pozas de Loureza — circular por el río Tamuxe
region_intro: Hiking around Oia offers diverse terrain, characterized by river valleys, coastal paths, and historical sites. The region features routes along the Río Tamuxe, trails with views of the Atlantic Ocean, and paths connecting traditional villages. These routes provide varied landscapes, from forested sections to open coastal stretches. The area's network includes paths leading to medieval fountains and traditional mills. Best hiking trails around Oia The most popular hiking route is Poza de Arco to Pozas de Loureza — circular by the Río Tamuxe, a 6.0 miles (9.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This route follows the Río Tamuxe through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Medieval Fountain in Baiona – O Muíño Mill loop from Regato das Pías, a difficult 17.5 miles (28.1 km) path. This trail leads through historical sites like a medieval fountain and O Muíño Mill. Local hikers also love the O Muíño Mill – View of the Atlantic Ocean loop from Pedra Rubia, a 9.3 miles (15.0 km) trail leading through coastal scenery, often completed in about 3 hours 59 minutes. Hiking around Oia is defined by river valleys, coastal views, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Oia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 290 reviews. More than 1,800 hikers have used komoot to explore Oia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Oia?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails around Oia, offering diverse experiences from moderate walks to more challenging routes. The komoot community has explored these routes over 2,700 times, providing an average rating of 4.6 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Oia's hiking trails?
A: Hiking around Oia features dramatic caldera views and unique volcanic landscapes. Trails vary from paved sections within villages to gravel and dirt paths along cliffside stretches. You'll experience continuous panoramic views of the Aegean Sea and the iconic Santorini caldera.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Oia?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. One popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375270160/poza-de-arco-to-pozas-de-loureza-circular-by-the-rio-tamuxe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza de Arco to Pozas de Loureza — circular by the Río Tamuxe&lt;/a&gt;, a 6.0-mile (9.7 km) trail following the Río Tamuxe. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38175292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Traditional Galician Hórreo loop from Oia&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 12.7 km (7.9 miles).

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking near Oia?
A: The trails offer breathtaking panoramic views of the Aegean Sea and the Santorini caldera. You can also discover unique geological formations shaped by the island's volcanic nature. For specific natural attractions, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3124664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozas de Loureza Natural Pools and Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3124657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loureza Pools (Bogalleira)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls near Oia?
A: Yes, you can find beautiful waterfalls and natural pools near Oia. Notable spots include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3124664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozas de Loureza Natural Pools and Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6093427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall and Natural Pool of the Cal River&lt;/a&gt;. These offer refreshing stops along your hike.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Oia?
A: While the guide does not specify dog-friendly routes, many trails in Oia are natural paths and suitable for dogs, especially those outside the busiest village centers. Always ensure your dog is on a leash and respect local regulations and other hikers. It's advisable to carry enough water for both yourself and your pet.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Oia?
A: The best seasons for hiking in Oia are typically spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these months, the weather is milder, and the crowds are generally smaller than in the peak summer season. The sea breeze provides some relief, but direct sun exposure can be intense.

Q: How difficult are the hikes around Oia?
A: The hikes around Oia cater to various fitness levels. There are 7 easy routes, 11 moderate routes, and 4 difficult routes. The famous Oia-Fira trail, for example, is generally considered moderate, with some uphill climbs and rocky sections. You can find options suitable for both seasoned hikers and casual walkers.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Oia?
A: The trails in Oia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the continuous, breathtaking panoramic views of the Aegean Sea and the iconic Santorini caldera, as well as the unique volcanic landscapes and charming whitewashed villages along the way.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles to explore near Oia?
A: Yes, the region around Oia is rich in history. You can explore several castles and fortresses. Consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5111769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monterreal Castle (Monte do Boi Fortress)&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6966613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baiona Parador and Monterreal Fortress&lt;/a&gt;. These sites offer historical context and often provide expansive views.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Oia?
A: Yes, Oia offers 7 easy hiking routes that are suitable for beginners or families. These trails often feature less challenging terrain and shorter distances, allowing everyone to enjoy the stunning caldera views and picturesque villages without extensive effort. Look for routes with lower elevation changes for a more relaxed experience.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Oia?
A: Parking in Oia can be challenging, especially during peak season. There are public parking areas at the entrance to Oia village, but these can fill up quickly. For specific trailheads, parking availability varies. It's often recommended to arrive early or consider using public transport where possible to avoid parking difficulties.

Q: Can I reach hiking trailheads by public transport in Oia?
A: Public transport, primarily buses, connects Oia with other villages like Fira, which is a common starting or ending point for the caldera rim hike. While buses can get you to the general vicinity, some specific trailheads might require a short walk from the nearest bus stop. Always check local bus schedules for the most up-to-date information.
tips: Wooden bridge that crosses the Tamuxe River above the natural pools that its descent creates towards the south towards the Miño River. Enjoy the river scenery and swim on warm summer days.

The high pools of the Loureza River (Bogalleira) are a stunning place to enjoy wild swimming at its finest. The water of the Tamuxe River flows through this valley to create this beautiful environment.

Impressive natural pools in the river. There are multiple locations to enter the river, some of them deep enough to allow jumping; and many small waterfalls. There's some parking along the nearby road.
